title: Source lineage for Scottish Alliterative Poems in Riming Stanzas (Amours five-poem text selection)
summary: Lineage for the editor-defined five-poem alt5980 selection, separating repository XML, U-M encoding, 1966 carrier, 1897 edition, five abstract works, ten represented streams, witnesses, facsimiles, access, cache, and rights layers.
review_notes: The subject is an edited selection, not one medieval work or the whole volume.
Five works are counted once; ten streams remain representation layers.
Historical text, witness matter, editorial and encoding work, image rights, physical custody, and provider terms are separate.

rights.component: Repository-authored XML serialization, data structure, and markup layer
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: CME's Unlicense text is expressly framed for software; no evidence establishes a blanket grant over inherited XML, data, or markup, so the repository-authored layer remains unknown.

rights.component: Target text represented from Amours's 1897 foreign edition
rights.basis: The artifact represents target text published by Amours in 1897. Section 104A(a)(1)(B) limits any restored copyright to the remainder of the U.S. term, and section 304 caps the relevant term at 95 years; even maximum protection ended no later than 1992. This does not clear XML markup, source-country rights, images, or unpublished witness matter.

rights.component: Modern electronic encoding, selection, and markup layer
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The current U-M item exposes the encoding but does not establish a transferable license for the modern encoding and markup layer; provider terms therefore remain applicable.

rights.component: Amours-derived historical text represented by the U-M encoding
rights.basis: The encoded target text derives from the 1897 Amours publication. Under sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304, even maximum U.S. protection ended no later than 1992. This row excludes the modern encoding layer and unconcorded unpublished witness matter.

rights.component: 1966 Johnson reprint carrier and item layer
rights.basis: The exact U-M/DPLA item currently carries the No Copyright-United States statement. This is item- and jurisdiction-specific and is not inferred merely from the 1966 date.

rights.component: Underlying 1897 Amours edition content carried by the 1966 reprint
rights.basis: The carried edition was published in 1897. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 limit any restored U.S. term to no later than 1992. The 1966 carrier layer remains separately described by RT05.

rights.component: 1897 editorial text, arrangement, and historical apparatus
rights.basis: Amours's edition was published abroad in 1897. Under sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304, any restored U.S. copyright could run only for the remaining U.S. term, capped at 95 years, ending no later than 1992. No source-country conclusion is made.

rights.component: Open Library API metadata cache
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The cached API record supplies bibliography but no component-specific reuse grant for the metadata layer; Open Library provider terms remain applicable.

rights.component: Internet Archive and Google scan derivatives and delivery layer
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The cached derivatives do not supply an open component-specific licence for the modern scan and delivery layer. Embedded delivery notices and provider terms are not treated as historical-text copyright conclusions.

rights.component: Underlying 1897 Amours edition visible in the scan
rights.basis: Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap any restored U.S. term for the 1897 publication at no later than 1992. This row excludes the modern scan and delivery layer.

rights.component: Five-poem historical text selection as exposed by U-M; new project catalog wording excluded
rights.basis: The exact U-M item labels the exposed historical material No Copyright-United States. The status is not extended to new catalog prose, modern interfaces, independent witnesses, or other jurisdictions.

rights.component: DIMEV v1.0 dataset and descriptive records
rights.basis: The DIMEV v1.0 release identifies its data as licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International; the licence applies to the dataset layer, not third-party witnesses or facsimiles.

rights.component: Provisional aggregate description of modern working transcripts and collations
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: Amours's acknowledgements establish a class of editorial intermediaries but not one surviving object, owner, repository, access endpoint, or licence. No access_id is assigned and no rights conclusion is invented.

rights.component: Target historical work text as represented in Amours's 1897 publication
rights.basis: The claimed text is limited to the historical work representation published by Amours in 1897. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap any restored U.S. term at no later than 1992. Modern METS editorial apparatus is excluded.

rights.component: Target historical work text as represented in Amours's 1897 publication
rights.basis: The same-entity route reaches the Amours text. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap any restored U.S. term for the 1897 publication at no later than 1992. Unpublished witness matter and modern editorial layers are excluded.

rights.component: Target historical work text as represented in Amours's 1897 publication
rights.basis: The claimed layer is limited to the historical text published by Amours in 1897. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap any restored U.S. term at no later than 1992. Modern METS apparatus is excluded.

rights.component: Target historical work text as represented in Amours's 1897 publication
rights.basis: The claimed layer is limited to the historical text published by Amours in 1897. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap any restored U.S. term at no later than 1992. Modern METS apparatus and unpublished witness matter are excluded.

rights.component: Target historical work text as represented in Amours's 1897 publication
rights.basis: The claimed layer is limited to the historical text streams published by Amours in 1897. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap any restored U.S. term at no later than 1992. Modern METS apparatus and other witness matter are excluded.

rights.component: Target text of the 1508 printed witness; physical ownership excluded
rights.basis: The target text was published in 1508. Even assuming restoration analysis under sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304, no U.S. term could remain. This does not clear modern images or catalog data.

rights.component: NLS catalog metadata cache
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The NLS record provides descriptive metadata but no component-specific open licence for the cached catalog layer; provider terms remain applicable.

rights.component: Wikimedia Commons Chepman and Myllar DjVu derivative
rights.basis: The exact Commons file is marked public domain. That statement applies to the named file and does not silently govern other provider images or catalog layers.

rights.component: Only the Howlat target passage transcribed and published by Amours in 1897
rights.basis: Amours directly transcribed the Asloan target stream and published it in 1897. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap any restored U.S. term at no later than 1992. This row does not clear all text in MS 16500 or any image layer.

rights.component: NLS Asloan manuscript images
rights.basis: The exact item carries a Public Domain Mark and NLS's current policy applies a CC0 waiver/no-known-restrictions statement to faithful digitization. The assertion is item-specific.

rights.component: Full underlying Bannatyne witness text; published collated readings excluded from this row
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: Amours records collated Bannatyne variants rather than a complete transcription. The 1897 publication makes those published readings separately usable at the edition layer, but it does not establish that the full underlying witness text is published or cleared.

rights.component: NLS Bannatyne Volume I images; no alt5980 target overlap
rights.basis: The exact Volume I item carries a Public Domain Mark and NLS's current CC0 waiver/no-known-restrictions policy. Target absence affects relevance, not the item-specific image statement.

rights.component: NLS Bannatyne Volume II images
rights.basis: The exact Volume II item carries a Public Domain Mark and NLS's current CC0 waiver/no-known-restrictions policy. The assertion is item-specific and distinct from underlying-text status.

rights.component: Target text of the circa-1508 split print fragment
rights.basis: The target fragment was published circa 1508. Even assuming restoration analysis under sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304, no U.S. term could remain. Modern photography and unresolved physical unity are excluded.

rights.component: Future Cambridge-supplied fragment photography and reuse
rights.basis: Cambridge's imaging route reserves item- and use-specific supply and licensing decisions. No open licence exists for future images that have not yet been supplied.

rights.component: Future Dundee-supplied fragment photography and reuse
rights.basis: Dundee's reproduction service conditions image supply and reuse on provider review and permission. No open licence is inferred for unsupplied future images.

rights.component: Target text of the 1572 printed witness; physical ownership excluded
rights.basis: The target text was published in 1572. Even assuming restoration analysis under sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304, no U.S. term could remain. Modern images, microfilm, and catalog data are excluded.

rights.component: NLS catalog metadata cache
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The NLS record supplies exact bibliographic and access data but no component-specific open licence for the cached catalog layer.

rights.component: Future NLS-supplied print photography and reuse
rights.basis: NLS copying and copyright guidance separates the cost and supply of a copy from permission to reuse it; future images require item- and use-specific review.

rights.component: Only the Awntyrs target passage transcribed and published by Amours in 1897
rights.basis: Amours directly transcribed the Douce target stream and published it in 1897. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap any restored U.S. term at no later than 1992. This row excludes all other Douce 324 matter and image layers.

rights.component: Bodleian catalog TEI cache
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The catalog TEI supplies descriptive evidence but no component-specific open reuse grant is established for the cached catalog layer.

rights.component: Future Bodleian whole-codex or reference photography and reuse
rights.basis: Bodleian imaging services condition supply and reuse on item- and use-specific terms; the dated public search found no complete public target sequence and no open licence is inferred for future images.

rights.component: Two Digital Bodleian slide images
rights.basis: The exact Digital Bodleian object publishes its two images under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0. The licence covers those images only, not a complete witness facsimile.

rights.component: Only the Awntyrs target passage transcribed and published by Amours in 1897
rights.basis: Amours directly transcribed the Lincoln target stream and published it in 1897. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap any restored U.S. term at no later than 1992. Other witness matter, custody, and reproduction terms are excluded.

rights.component: Only the Awntyrs target passage transcribed and published by Robson in 1842
rights.basis: Robson directly transcribed the Taylor target stream and published it abroad in 1842. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap any restored U.S. term at no later than 1937. Other witness matter and modern images are excluded.

rights.component: Princeton microfilm image layer
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The exact provider object is marked Copyright Not Evaluated and supplies no open licence. CNE is a cautionary rights statement, not permission to reuse.

rights.component: 1842 edition text, arrangement, and historical apparatus
rights.basis: Robson's edition was published abroad in 1842. Under sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304, any restored U.S. term could last no later than 1937. No source-country or modern-scan conclusion is made.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan derivatives and delivery layer
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The cached scan derivatives do not establish an open component-specific licence for the modern scan and delivery layer; provider terms remain applicable.

rights.component: Underlying 1842 Robson edition visible in the scan
rights.basis: Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap any restored U.S. term for the 1842 publication at no later than 1937. This row excludes the modern scan and delivery layer.

rights.component: Underlying text of the Lambeth Awntyrs witness
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: Lambeth MS 491 is a collateral witness with no Amours or Robson source edge and no demonstrated old publication of its exact target passage. Medieval age alone is not a sufficient U.S. rights basis, so the underlying witness text remains unknown.

rights.component: Lambeth catalog HTML cache
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The exact catalog page provides descriptive evidence but no component-specific open reuse licence for the cached HTML layer.

rights.component: Future Lambeth research photography and publication or reuse
rights.basis: Lambeth Imaging Services separates research-photography supply from publication permission and fees. No open licence is inferred for future images.

rights.component: Only the Pistill target passage represented through Amours's published 1897 working-set transcription
rights.basis: Amours's published edition and acknowledgements support a Vernon-derived target stream through the aggregate editorial intermediary. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap the 1897 publication's U.S. term at no later than 1992. Other Vernon matter is excluded.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian Vernon images
rights.basis: The exact Digital Bodleian object publishes its images under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0. The licence governs the image layer, not the physical manuscript or other provider layers.

rights.component: Only the Pistill target passage represented through Amours's published 1897 working-set transcription
rights.basis: Amours's published edition and acknowledgements support an Additional 22283-derived target stream through the aggregate editorial intermediary. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap the 1897 publication's U.S. term at no later than 1992. Other witness matter is excluded.

rights.component: Future British Library photography and reuse for Additional 22283
rights.basis: The British Library digitisation route conditions future image supply and reuse on provider review; no open licence is inferred for unsupplied images.

rights.component: Only the Pistill target passage represented through Amours's published 1897 working-set transcription
rights.basis: Amours's published edition and acknowledgements support a Cotton-derived target stream through the aggregate editorial intermediary. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap the 1897 publication's U.S. term at no later than 1992. Missing stanzas and other witness matter are excluded.

rights.component: Future British Library photography and reuse for Cotton Caligula A.II
rights.basis: The British Library digitisation route conditions future image supply and reuse on provider review; no open licence is inferred for unsupplied images.

rights.component: Only the Pistill target passage represented through Amours's published 1897 working-set transcription
rights.basis: Amours's published edition and acknowledgements support a Morgan-derived target stream through the aggregate editorial intermediary. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap the 1897 publication's U.S. term at no later than 1992. Other Morgan matter is excluded.

rights.component: Morgan low-resolution published images
rights.basis: Morgan permits the published low-resolution images for personal, educational, or noncommercial private research while requiring Imaging and Rights permission for publication or commercial reuse. The private cache is not a redistributable corpus asset.

rights.component: Only the Pistill target passage represented through Amours's published 1897 working-set transcription
rights.basis: Amours's published edition and acknowledgements support a Huntington-derived target stream through the aggregate editorial intermediary. Sections 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304 cap the 1897 publication's U.S. term at no later than 1992. Other Huntington matter is excluded.

rights.component: Huntington digital image layer
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest has a null licence field and the provider supplies no open component-specific reuse licence. Provider policy applies and no permission is inferred.

open_questions.question: Which date within the late-fourteenth to 1420s range best fits Awntyrs?
Can Awntyrs be securely identified with Huchown's Gret Gest?
next_steps: Do not treat the identification as settled.

open_questions.question: Does Wyntoun securely identify Huchown as Pistill's author?
Do Golagros and Rauf share an author or support historical attributions?
next_steps: Keep both anonymous pending stronger evidence.

open_questions.question: What are the exact shelfmarks, unity, split history, and facsimile availability of the Cambridge/Dundee fragment?
next_steps: Ask both repositories to reconcile holdings.

open_questions.question: Is DIMEV's different-incipit Sloane 1098 item relevant to this Howlat text?
next_steps: Compare before promoting it to a witness.

open_questions.question: How should the f. 213r versus f. 213v Asloan start disagreement be reconciled?
next_steps: Preserve both; use visually inspected f. 213v.

open_questions.question: What is the exact Douce locus, and can a complete whole-codex surrogate be obtained?
Do Amours's working materials survive, and which supplier handled each witness?
next_steps: Search archives; do not invent mappings.

open_questions.question: Can complete public target sequences be located for the six missing sources?
next_steps: Recheck catalogs and preserve request routes.

open_questions.question: Can the 610-image U-M carrier bundle be obtained through an authorized route?
next_steps: Ask U-M; do not bypass HTTP 403.
